content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Found 2 correctness gaps: planned-elsewhere reconstruction can replay stale historical aggregates into the current reconciliation, and the reviewer prompt does not actually instruct reviewers to emit the new `mapped_to_bead_id` classification.

## Amendments

1. [success_reconciliation.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs#L871) reconstructs missing planned-elsewhere mappings by scanning every `final_review` aggregate payload in the project history ([success_reconciliation.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs#L882), [success_reconciliation.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs#L913)). Because this code is not scoped to the current run or the latest committed review round, a later successful reconciliation can resurrect stale mappings from superseded restart rounds, older runs, or abandoned review attempts and then persist/comment them as if they still applied. Restrict reconstruction to the current run’s committed final-review outcome before recording any mapping.
   Rationale: This can attach the wrong finding to the milestone journal and post misleading comments on unrelated beads after the active bead has already changed or resolved the concern.
2. The new `mapped_to_bead_id` field has no reliable producer in the reviewer stage. `build_reviewer_prompt` still renders the generic reviewer template ([final_review.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs#L1838), [template_catalog.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs#L220)) and that template never explains planned-elsewhere classification or asks reviewers to return a bead id. The only new prompt text about planned-elsewhere was added downstream in the voter/arbiter prompts ([final_review.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs#L1880), [final_review.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs#L1937)), after `mapped_to_bead_id` would already need to exist. Add explicit reviewer instructions for when to emit `mapped_to_bead_id` and how to choose it.
   Rationale: Without reviewer-side guidance, the field will usually stay `None`, so planned-elsewhere findings keep flowing through the ordinary amendment path and still trigger restart loops.
